characters.name: Achoo
characters.description: AchooFull NameAchooSpeciesDemonPowers / SkillsShapeshiftingEnemiesDuck DodgersFirst AppearanceSamurai Quack Achoois a powerful wizard and the main antagonist of the episode,Samurai Quack. He is an alternate form ofDuck Dodgers' alarm clock,Happy Catas well as a parody of Aku, the main antagonist of the animated television series,Samurai Jack. Much like his SJ counterpart, Achoo is voiced byMako Iwamatsu, who also voices Happy Cat.

characters.name: Cassiopeia
characters.description: CassiopeiaFull NameCassiopeia Sandra ReissSpeciesHumanHomeworldEarthFamilyMike Reiss (father)Jessica Silverman (mother)Nina(sister)FriendsDuck DodgersFirst AppearanceThey Stole Dodgers' BrainLast AppearanceThey Stole Dodgers' BrainVoiced byJane Wiedlin Cassiopeiais a singer who appears in the episode "They Stole Dodgers' Brain". Cassiopeia has fair skin and long flowing blonde hair that is tied at the end. Not much is known about Cassiopeia, but she seems mostly nice. However, after her experience with Dodgers, she is furious at him. They do make up however, and is shown to be happy while listening to him.

characters.name: Commander X-2
characters.description: Commander X-2SpeciesMartianHomeworldMarsOccupationCommanderFamilyUnnamed parentsK-9(pet)FriendsQueen Tyr'ahnee,Martian Centurion,Duck Dodgers(sometimes),Space Cadet(sometimes),EnemiesDuck Dodgers,Space Cadet,Ignatius Q "I.Q." Hi Martian Commander X-2 Marvin, better known asCommander X-2, is the secondary antagonist ofDuck Dodgers. He is the commander of theMartian militaryas well asDuck Dodgers' arch enemy. He has a crush on thethe Martian Queen, whom he serves without question. Despite their many battles, he considers Duck Dodgers to be more of a nuisance than an actual enemy. Commander X-2 usually presents himself as a figure well deserving of his standing, often concocting deep strategies and attack plans to benefit the Martian Empire and his Queen. In contrast to this, his actual personality can best be described as courteous, reserved and generally nonthreatening, which often leads people to rarely take him seriously. This often exasperates the Commander to the point of anger and hostility, which often causes his plans to fly off the handle. He also nurses a phobia of heights, which he claims resulted from his father dangling him from a balcony to attract the media. He is also openly thoughtful of his Mother, a fact that is implied to annoy her, and often causes his own troops to talk about him behind his back. The Commander is deeply infatuated with Queen Tyr'ahnee, a fact she seems oblivious to, but the two nonetheless share an open trusting friendship in addition to their positions as Royalty and Servitude. Despite this, she is not above abusing him should his plans backfire and embarrass the Empire. Though the two share a mutual hatred for each other, the Commander is not above working with Dodgers should the situation call for cooperation, and he is usually the first to recommend it. In contrast, he tends to get along much better with the Cadet, whom he sees as a much more reasonable person.

characters.name: Counselor Combustion
characters.description: Counselor Combustion(voiced by Jennifer Hale) is a lawyer who was hired by the Martian to convince that Hungortus should eat Earth. She hates her brother, Flame Valet. She was then moved to Dodgers' side.

characters.name: Drake Darkstar
characters.description: Drake Darkstaris a criminal mastermind who resemblesDuck Dodgers(except that the feathers on top of his head are tousled backwards, instead of forwards like Dodgers). During a prison visit, Drake escaped and the guards took Dodgers into custody, mistaking him for Drake. When Dodgers and Drake fought in front of the guards, their captain and Cadet, Cadet chose Drake because “he was much nicer to him” because Dodgers absentmindedly revealed that he sold Cadet’s sister to the sausage factory, an act that even Darkstar considered “cold”, which Darkstar may draws a line on something likes that. The only way to tell him apart was his British accent and evil smile. Like Dodgers, Darkstar was also voiced byJoe Alaskey.

characters.name: Duck Dodgers
characters.description: Duck DodgersFull NameDuck Edgar Dumas Aloysius Eoghain DodgersSpeciesDuckHomeworldEarthFamilyUnnamed GrandmotherUnnamed MotherUnnamed BrotherFriendsSpace Cadet,Ignatius Q "I.Q." HiEnemiesQueen Tyr'ahnee,Commander X-2,Martian Centurion,Instant Martians,General Z-9First AppearanceThe Trial of Duck DodgersLast AppearanceBonafide Hero: Captain Duck Dodgers Captain Duck Edgar Dumas Aloysius Eoghain Dodgers, better known asDuck Dodgers, is the titular protagonist ofDuck Dodgers. Dodgers was a poor, helpless soul that was accidentally frozen for about 351 years. He was later revived byIgnatius Q "I.Q." Hiin the 24½th Century (around the year 2372). Through scheming and lies, he managed to trick most of the populace into believing he was a 21st Century hero, when in reality, he was only the water-boy for a football team (incidentally, however,Commander X-2accidentally caused Dodgers to win a championship football game, so he was not technically lying concerning that). He mainly relies on luck and the work of theSpace Cadet, but has occasionally shown fairly high levels of heroism and competence, leading us to the conclusion that sometimes, he isn't as daft as we think. Though he doesn't show it often, Dodgers cares deeply for his cadet, even though he often demeans and puts him through humiliating situations.
